name: first-cooking-with-fire
description: Early hominins began cooking food over fire, making nutrients easier to digest and fueling brain growth.

name: wild-grain-harvesting
description: Hunter-gatherers in the Near East began gathering and grinding wild cereal grains long before farming.

name: first-bread-baked
description: Hunter-gatherers in the Near East baked flatbread from wild grains thousands of years before farming began.

name: barley-domesticated
description: Farmers in the Fertile Crescent domesticated barley, a staple grain used for food and early brewing.

name: first-beer-brewed
description: Early farmers in the Near East and China fermented grains into beer, one of humanity's oldest drinks.

name: goat-milk-dairying
description: Neolithic farmers in the Near East began milking domesticated goats and cattle, starting dairy farming.

name: chickens-domesticated
description: Wild jungle fowl were domesticated in Southeast Asia, becoming the world's most common food bird.

name: first-wine-making
description: People in the Caucasus region fermented grapes into wine, beginning a tradition spread across Eurasia.

name: olive-cultivation-begins
description: Mediterranean farmers began cultivating olive trees for oil, a cornerstone of regional diet and trade.

name: honey-collecting-depicted
description: Rock art in Spain shows people climbing to gather wild honey, humanity's earliest sweetener.

name: first-sugarcane-cultivation
description: People in New Guinea began cultivating sugarcane, chewing the stalks for their sweet juice.

name: salt-evaporation-ponds
description: Early communities harvested salt by evaporating seawater and mining deposits to preserve and flavor food.

name: bananas-cultivated
description: Farmers in New Guinea cultivated bananas, one of the earliest domesticated fruit crops.

name: first-cheese-making
description: Neolithic dairy farmers curdled milk into early cheese, preserving nutrition long after milking.

name: beekeeping-in-egypt
description: Ancient Egyptians kept bees in clay hives, harvesting honey as a sweetener, medicine, and offering.

name: egyptian-leavened-bread
description: Ancient Egyptians used yeast to bake the first leavened, risen bread, perfecting the baking craft.

name: first-recipes-written
description: Babylonian clay tablets recorded the world's oldest known recipes, detailing stews and broths.

name: tea-discovered-china
description: Tradition holds that tea was first brewed in ancient China, becoming a defining drink of the region.

name: soybean-domesticated
description: Farmers in ancient China domesticated the soybean, a protein-rich crop central to East Asian cuisine.

name: chocolate-in-mesoamerica
description: Mesoamerican peoples ground cacao beans into a bitter, frothy drink prized in rituals and trade.

name: garum-fish-sauce
description: Romans produced garum, a fermented fish sauce used as a popular seasoning across the empire.

name: first-cookbook-apicius
description: The Roman text Apicius compiled elaborate recipes, becoming one of the oldest surviving cookbooks.

name: vineyards-spread-rome
description: The Roman Empire planted vineyards across Europe, spreading wine production from Spain to Britain.

name: watermelon-cultivated-egypt
description: Ancient Egyptians cultivated watermelons, storing the water-rich fruit and depicting it in tombs.

name: rice-paddy-cultivation
description: Farmers in China developed flooded paddy fields for rice, boosting yields and supporting dense populations.

name: spice-trade-incense-route
description: Arabian and Indian traders moved cinnamon, pepper, and spices west, linking distant cuisines and economies.

name: noodles-in-china
description: Ancient Chinese cooks made noodles from millet, leaving the earliest known evidence of the dish.

name: first-cabbage-cultivation
description: Europeans cultivated wild cabbage, breeding it into kale, broccoli, and many related vegetables.

name: tofu-invented-china
description: Chinese cooks created tofu by curdling soy milk, adding a versatile protein to East Asian cuisine.

name: coffee-origins-ethiopia
description: Coffee plants native to Ethiopia were brewed into a stimulating drink that spread across the Arab world.

name: yemen-coffee-cultivation
description: Sufi communities in Yemen brewed coffee to stay awake for prayer, spreading the drink across Arabia.

name: pasta-dried-sicily
description: Arab-influenced cooks in Sicily produced dried pasta, allowing the storable food to be traded widely.

name: champagne-region-wine
description: Monasteries in the Champagne region of France developed prized vineyards centuries before sparkling wine.

name: windmills-grind-grain
description: European communities built windmills to grind grain into flour, mechanizing a vital food task.

name: medieval-guild-bakers
description: England's Assize of Bread set legal weights and prices for loaves to protect customers from cheating bakers.

name: marco-polo-asian-foods
description: Returning from Asia, Marco Polo described foods like noodles and exotic spices to fascinated Europeans.

name: aztec-chinampa-farming
description: The Aztecs built chinampas, fertile floating garden plots in lake shallows that fed their capital city.

name: columbian-exchange-food
description: Columbus's voyages began a global swap of crops and animals, moving potatoes, maize, and more worldwide.

name: tomato-reaches-europe
description: Spanish explorers brought the tomato from the Americas to Europe, where it slowly entered local cuisine.

name: chili-peppers-spread
description: Traders carried American chili peppers across Asia and Africa, transforming cuisines from India to Korea.

name: turkey-reaches-europe
description: Spanish explorers brought the domesticated turkey from the Americas to Europe as a new food bird.

name: potato-reaches-spain
description: Spanish ships carried the Andean potato to Europe, where it became a staple crop centuries later.

name: first-european-coffeehouse
description: Venice opened one of Europe's first coffeehouses, sparking a social culture around the new drink.

name: london-coffeehouse-opens
description: London's first coffeehouse opened, beginning hubs where merchants and thinkers gathered to trade news.

name: champagne-sparkling-wine
description: French winemakers in Champagne refined methods to trap bubbles, creating celebrated sparkling wine.

name: tea-craze-britain
description: Imported Chinese tea became a daily British drink, fueling vast trade and afternoon tea customs.

name: champagne-region-bubbly
description: Monk Dom Pérignon advanced winemaking techniques in Champagne that shaped its famous sparkling style.

name: gin-craze-london
description: Cheap gin flooded London, causing widespread drunkenness and prompting laws to curb its sale.

name: first-restaurant-paris
description: A Paris establishment offered restorative broths and menu choices, pioneering the modern restaurant.

name: potato-embraced-europe
description: Once feared, the potato became a vital European staple, championed by reformers to fight famine.

name: sandwich-named
description: The Earl of Sandwich popularized meat between bread so he could eat without leaving the gambling table.

name: canning-food-preservation
description: Nicolas Appert developed sealing food in jars, and Britain patented the tin can to preserve food longer.

name: first-chocolate-bar
description: British firm J.S. Fry produced the first molded eating chocolate bar, turning the drink into a candy.

name: margarine-invented
description: A French chemist created margarine as a cheap butter substitute, winning a prize set by Napoleon III.

name: milk-chocolate-invented
description: Swiss makers combined chocolate with condensed milk, creating the creamy milk chocolate eaten today.

name: mechanical-refrigeration-meat
description: Refrigerated ships first carried frozen meat across oceans, transforming the global food trade.

name: first-breakfast-cereal
description: The Kellogg brothers created flaked breakfast cereal at a Michigan sanitarium, launching a food industry.

name: coca-cola-first-sold
description: Pharmacist John Pemberton mixed the first Coca-Cola syrup in Atlanta, creating an iconic soft drink.

name: phylloxera-wine-blight
description: An American insect destroyed most of Europe's vineyards before grafting onto resistant rootstock saved them.

name: first-hamburger-served
description: Ground beef patties served in buns gained popularity in the United States, becoming an iconic food.

name: ice-cream-cone-debut
description: The St. Louis World's Fair helped popularize the edible ice cream cone to a mass American audience.

name: instant-coffee-introduced
description: Soluble instant coffee reached the mass market, letting people brew a cup by adding hot water.

name: household-refrigerator-sold
description: The first electric household refrigerator went on sale, letting families safely store fresh food at home.

name: frozen-food-flash-freezing
description: Clarence Birdseye patented quick-freezing, locking in flavor and launching the frozen food industry.

name: sliced-bread-sold
description: A Missouri bakery first sold machine-sliced bread, an innovation that inspired the famous phrase.

name: chocolate-chip-cookie
description: Ruth Wakefield invented the chocolate chip cookie at her Toll House inn in Massachusetts.

name: supermarket-self-service
description: Piggly Wiggly opened in Memphis, letting shoppers pick their own groceries and pioneering the supermarket.

name: first-mcdonalds-franchise
description: Ray Kroc opened a franchised McDonald's, building a global fast food empire from a burger stand.

name: tv-dinner-launched
description: Swanson sold the first frozen TV dinner in a partitioned tray, reshaping convenient home eating.

name: green-revolution-begins
description: High-yield crop varieties and fertilizers vastly raised harvests, easing famine across the developing world.

name: microwave-meals-spread
description: Affordable countertop microwave ovens reached homes, changing how families reheated and cooked food.

name: instant-noodles-invented
description: Momofuku Ando created instant ramen in Japan, a cheap quick meal that became a global staple.

name: fair-trade-coffee-movement
description: The first fair trade label launched in the Netherlands, certifying ethical pay for coffee and crops.

name: starbucks-coffee-chain
description: Starbucks opened in Seattle, growing into a global chain that spread café coffee culture worldwide.

name: genetically-modified-crop
description: The Flavr Savr tomato became the first genetically modified food sold, opening debate over GM crops.

name: lab-grown-meat-tasted
description: Scientists unveiled a hamburger grown from cultured cattle cells, a step toward meat without slaughter.

name: plant-based-meat-mainstream
description: Plant-based burgers from Impossible and Beyond Meat reached fast food chains and supermarket shelves.

name: food-delivery-apps-rise
description: Smartphone apps let users order restaurant meals to their door, reshaping how people eat out.
